home.title: AI agent setup — in seconds
home.lead: AI Kit generates configuration for Claude Code and OpenCode for any project. One prompt, one binary — and your repo is ready for AI-assisted development.

what.title: Everything an AI agent needs to start working in your repo
what.sub: AI Kit produces CLAUDE.md, .claude/settings.json, opencode.json, agent definitions, and a .planning/ structure that match the ai-agent-kit workflow. Works with Kotlin, Java, Python, TypeScript, Go, Rust and more.

providers.title: Two providers
providers.body: Generate config for Claude Code, OpenCode, or both at once.

languages.title: Any stack
languages.body: Pick a language and (optionally) a framework — generation adapts to your project.

planning.title: Planning system
planning.body: Optional .planning/ directory with current task, morning report, and per-task notes.

agents.title: Agent definitions
agents.body: Pre-baked subagent files for repetitive engineering chores.

quickstart.title: Quick Start
quickstart.body: The recommended way — paste a single prompt into Claude Code or OpenCode and let the agent do the rest.

cli.title: CLI Usage
cli.body: Prefer running things yourself? Download the binary and call it directly with flags.

files.title: Generated Files
files.body: See exactly what AI Kit creates in your repository and what each file does.

build.title: Build from Source
build.body: Compile the binary yourself with Gradle and Kotlin/Native.

quickstart.title: From zero to a configured project
quickstart.lead: The fastest way: paste a single prompt into Claude Code or OpenCode inside your project.
quickstart.step1: Open Claude Code or OpenCode in your project directory.
quickstart.step2: Paste the prompt below and let the agent take it from there.
quickstart.step3: The agent will ask about project name, language, framework, provider and model. It downloads the binary, runs it, and confirms the generated files.
quickstart.tip: Re-running the kit overwrites existing files safely — feel free to tweak answers and run again.

cli.title: CLI Usage
cli.lead: Prefer running the binary yourself? Download it from the Releases page and call it directly.

flagsList.name: Project name
flagsList.lang: Programming language (kotlin / java / python / typescript / go / rust / other)

files.title: Generated Files
files.lead: What lands in your project after running the kit.

purposes.claudemd: Top-level guidance Claude Code reads on every session.
purposes.claudeSettings: Permissions, env vars, and hooks for Claude Code.
purposes.claudeAgents: Subagent definitions used by Claude Code.
purposes.morning: Daily summary of what changed and what's next.

build.title: Build from Source
build.lead: If you want to compile the binary yourself, you'll need JDK 11+.

faq.title: Frequently asked questions
faq.lead: Quick answers to common questions about AI Kit.

items.q: Does the kit overwrite existing files?
items.a: Yes. It safely overwrites files it manages. If you have a customised CLAUDE.md, back it up before re-running.
Both. Generating both Claude Code and OpenCode configs is cheap and lets you switch tools without redoing setup.

items.q: What if my language isn't in the list?
items.a: Pass --lang other and skip --framework. Generation falls back to a generic template you can refine.
Open an issue on the GitHub repository — link in the footer.
